speaker: interviewee
text: --of history. So-- SUSANA (WIFE): Yeah? Yeah.

speaker: interviewee
text: It's important enough-- SUSANA (WIFE): You are-- you are in history, in--

speaker: interviewer
text: Yeah, I like history.

speaker: interviewee
text: You know, I passed two more of 10 universities.

speaker: interviewer
text: Excuse me?

speaker: interviewee
text: I passed two more of 10 universities. How, what, all the-- all the stuff, it's not easy.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture shows that I was with the officer from the 82nd-- SUSANA (WIFE): Division. --division. In Ludwig's [INAUDIBLE]. His name is Jerry Adler. He is a officer in the American army, in 82nd Division.

speaker: interviewer
text: And what year was this photographed?

speaker: interviewee
text: This was-- this the photograph in 1945, and the date will be around-- [PAUSES FOR 3 SECONDS] --May 10, May 12, something like that.

speaker: interviewer
text: Right after liberation?

speaker: interviewee
text: Right after-- no, I met him-- I met him-- I met this gentleman the 4th of May, before the end of war. And the war end May 9. And I met him before we got together, he and the whole-- the whole crew of his.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK. This picture shows me in center, my wife on my right side. My daughter, which is bat mitzvah. She is 13 years old. And it was in 1979. And my son, Brian. Brian Reifer. But you didn't tell us the name of your wife and your daughter.

speaker: interviewee
text: My wife's name is Susana Reifer. My daughter's name, the bar mitzvah daughter, is Anna Marey Reifer. And my son is Brian Reifer.

speaker: interviewer
text: And where was this photo-- what city was this photograph taken in?

speaker: interviewee
text: City, it was taken in Los Angeles. SUSANA (WIFE): In Encino. In Encino, California. And it-- I think this was taken-- and Stephen-- no, this was taken in--

speaker: interviewer
text: That he said this was at Valley Beth Shalom.

speaker: interviewee
text: Valley Beth Shalom, taken. Yeah.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: --graduate.

speaker: interviewer
text: What year was this?

speaker: interviewer
text: Ready? Go ahead.

speaker: interviewee
text: This picture shows the same thing, my family. SUSANA (WIFE): Eight years ago. I am on the left side. My son, Brian Reifer, is next to me. My lovely wife, till 190 years. And she is Anna Marey-- Susie Reifer. And my lovely daughter, Anna Marey Reifer.

speaker: interviewer
text: Where was this photograph taken?

speaker: interviewee
text: This photograph, I think, was taken in my house.

speaker: interviewer
text: No, this was taken here in Los Angeles.

speaker: interviewee
text: In Los Angeles.

speaker: interviewer
text: California.

speaker: interviewee
text: California.

speaker: interviewer
text: And you know what year this was taken? 1988, perhaps? 1988.

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: The picture was taken--

speaker: interviewer
text: OK.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK. SUSANA (WIFE): At Stephen S. Wise Temple. At Stephen S. Wise Temple.

speaker: interviewee
text: Stephen S. Wise Temple.

speaker: interviewee
text: OK. SUSANA (WIFE): [INAUDIBLE]. OK, this is the last photograph. And now we're going to bring you and your wife on camera.

speaker: interviewee
text: That--

speaker: interviewer
text: OK, Mr. Reifer, this is the very end. We're finally at the finish. I see you have a really beautiful woman sitting next to you.

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you. Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: Why don't you introduce us to her?

speaker: interviewee
text: First of all, my name is Alex Reifer. And this is my lovely wife, Susana Reifer. The And we only married, not too long. From 11 of May, 1963. So a couple months ago. And we are very happy together. And we stay-- we hope to stay a couple hundred years more.

speaker: interviewer
text: I think because we know you're such a jokester, we better be very clear. So from 100 years from now, when someone may look at this tape, we should really say that today is June 22nd, 1996. And you have been married a little over 33 years. And 33 years and some months. So that was a little bit of a joke that you just made.

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you

speaker: interviewer
text: So that nobody thinks that you really were just married a couple of months. OK?

words.text: just married a couple of months. OK?

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you. Thank you. SUSANA (WIFE): I'm very proud, what my husband did during the war. And I'm very proud, what he kill so many Nazis. And he's a wonderful man. And he always help everybody. Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: Could you hear me on the microphone when I said the date? Because it's so-- I'm so off now. Mr. Reifer, I really can't tell you what an honor this has been, to meet you today, and your wife.

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you.

speaker: interviewer
text: And I know that you-- the stories you have are far greater than you were able to share with us. And perhaps someday you'll be able to share them with everyone else.

speaker: interviewee
text: I will.

speaker: interviewer
text: But I really do want to thank you for telling us as much as you told us today.

speaker: interviewee
text: Thank you. SUSANA (WIFE): Thank you.
